channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A boy with a loaded shotgun boarded a plane in Australia but was tackled by a former boxer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A 17-year-old boy with a loaded shotgun boarded a plane at an Australian airport before being restrained by a pilot and two passengers, police said Friday. The boy was disarmed and detained before police arrived on the scene Thursday at Avalon Airport in Victoria state.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: List of Social Security Administration offices DOGE is closing
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency has started to make large budget cuts, resulting in the closure of multiple SSA offices.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: San Carlos Apache teenager's death reverberates throughout Indian Country
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — From heartbreak and devastation to outrage, Emily Pike's tragic death is stirring heavy emotions and putting the spotlight to a crisis that has long plagued Native American communities, where a disproportionate number of people have been killed or have gone missing.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Florida residents record SpaceX explosion
item.description: Residents in Tampa Bay, Florida were confounded after seeing what seemed to be an explosion in the sky around Thursday's sunset.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: Judge says plans to release a woman in Slender Man case can go forward
item.description: WAUKESHA, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin woman who nearly killed her classmate years ago to please horror character Slender Man can be released from a psychiatric hospital as planned, a judge decided Thursday, rejecting state health officials’ last-minute attempt to keep her committed.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The controversy behind California's swastika-shaped building complex
item.description: The Navy Amphibious Base Coronado in San Diego County has been the subject of controversy for decades due to the exterior design of four barracks that resemble a swastika.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 men plead not guilty in drug overdose deaths of 3 Kansas City Chiefs fans after game
item.description: PLATTE CITY, Mo. (AP) — Two men pleaded not guilty Thursday in the drug overdose deaths of three Kansas City Chiefs fans whose bodies were found in a backyard two days after they got together to watch the final game of the regular season in 2024.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A South Carolina man is set to become the first executed by firing squad in 15 years
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — When the clock strikes 6 Friday evening, a South Carolina man will walk into the death chamber, be strapped into a chair and have a target placed over his heart.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Life-saving avalanche forecasts could be hindered by Trump budget cuts
item.description: LEADVILLE, Colo. (AP) — Officials in the Western U.S. who warn the public about avalanches are sounding a different type of alarm.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cockpit recorder likely hadn't worked 'for several years' before jet crashed in Philadelphia: NTSB
item.description: The cockpit voice recorder in the medical transport jet that slammed into a Philadelphia neighborhood in January had likely not worked for years, according to a preliminary report released by the National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: California landlords prohibited from charging these fees starting in April
item.description: The bill also prohibits landlords or agents from charging a tenant any fee for paying rent or security deposits by check.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk's PAC is running ads touting Trump's accomplishments
item.description: The political action committee that Elon Musk funded to the tune of more than $200 million on behalf of President Donald Trump launched a television advertisement this week lauding the first weeks of the administration in which the Tesla CEO is serving.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California company celebrates successful return of medical research space capsule
item.description: A Southern California company is celebrating the successful launch and return of a space capsule designed to change how pharmaceuticals are processed in Earth's low orbit. Varda Space Industries, based in El Segundo, launched its W-2 capsule aboard a Space X rocket in mid-January.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Facing Trump's threats, Columbia investigates students critical of Israel
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Columbia University senior Maryam Alwan was visiting family in Jordan over winter break when she received an email from the school accusing her of discriminatory harassment. Her supposed top offense: writing an op-ed in the student newspaper calling for divestment from Israel.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House votes to censure Al Green for disrupting Trump speech
item.description: The House voted Thursday to censure Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) for his protest during President Trump’s address to Congress this week — a disruptive display that prompted his removal from the House chamber.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: 3 skiers missing after avalanche hits remote mountain range in Alaska
item.description: Alaska State Troopers said three skiers are likely dead after an avalanche buried them in the Chugach Mountains near Girdwood, Alaska, on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope rests after peaceful night during third week of pneumonia treatment
item.description: Pope Francis was resting Thursday after a peaceful night during his third week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US charges Chinese hackers and government officials in a broad cybercrime campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Twelve Chinese nationals — including mercenary hackers, law enforcement officers and employees of a private hacking company — have been charged in connection with global cybercrime campaigns targeting dissidents, news organizations, U.S.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and DAKE KANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former New York detective convicted of lying to FBI to protect mafia family
item.description: A New York jury on Wednesday convicted a former detective of lying to authorities in an effort to protect the mafia family whom he moonlighted for.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tom Llamas to succeed Lester Holt as NBC "Nightly News" anchor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — NBC News appointed Tom Llamas on Wednesday to replace Lester Holt as anchor of “Nightly News” starting this summer, but he's not abandoning his streaming newscast. The elevation of Llamas, 45, was widely anticipated.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US travel advisory for Turks and Caicos Islands updated, year after Americans arrested
item.description: The updated advisory comes nearly a year after several Americans were arrested on ammunition charges in Turks and Caicos.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KRON4

item.title: Michigan brothers missing since 2010 declared dead at mom's request
item.description: A judge declared three Michigan brothers dead Wednesday, more than 14 years after they disappeared at Thanksgiving in a small-town tragedy that remains unsolved.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 31 million Americans borrowed money for health care last year: Poll
item.description: (The Hill) — More than 31 million Americans borrowed money last year to pay for health care, a new survey found. Those Americans borrowed about $74 billion, despite most of them have some form of health insurance, the West Health-Gallup survey found.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Boy reportedly kidnapped from Atlanta 7 years ago found in Colorado; 2 arrested
item.description: A 14-year-old boy who went missing seven years ago has been located, some 1,400 miles from where he was allegedly abducted.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, ABC 4

item.title: Powerful US storms kill 2 and bring threats from critical fire weather to blizzard conditions
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms killed two people in Mississippi, tore the roofs off an apartment building and a nursing home in a small town in Oklahoma and threatened more communities across the nation Tuesday with wide-ranging weather.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Elissa Slotkin assails Trump's early actions, offers Democrats a way to fight back
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — First-term Michigan Sen. Elissa Slotkin accused President Donald Trump of driving up costs while pushing for an “unprecedented giveaway to his billionaire friends” in Tuesday night's Democratic response to his first joint congressional address of his second term.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's address to Congress
item.description: President Trump delivered the first congressional address of his second term on Tuesday evening. It was a chance for the president to underscore the sweeping changes he has wrought in the six weeks since he returned to the White House — and to defend those moves from his many critics.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Powerful US storms kill 2 and bring threats from critical fire weather to blizzard conditions
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms killed two people in Mississippi, tore the roofs off an apartment building and a nursing home in a small town in Oklahoma and threatened more communities across the nation Tuesday with wide-ranging weather.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democrat Al Green removed from House after interrupting, heckling Trump
item.description: Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) was removed from the House chamber where President Trump was giving his address to a joint session of Congress after the congressman heckled the president early in his speech.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge tells attorneys to stop being so secretive in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder case
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An Idaho judge is warning attorneys to quit filing so many sealed documents in the murder case of a man accused in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Los Angeles fire chief fails to get job back after ouster following historic deadly wildfire
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The former Los Angeles fire chief who was ousted by Mayor Karen Bass after the most destructive wildfire in city history failed to win back her job Tuesday, falling short with her argument that her dismissal was based on false accusations about her conduct and decision-making.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Florida attorney general's office is investigating Andrew and Tristan Tate
item.description: Florida's attorney general announced Tuesday that his office has opened a criminal investigation into Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, days after they returned to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Why Trump's speech to Congress isn't a 'State of the Union' address
item.description: It looks like the State of the Union, and will be carried on live television, just like those annual addresses are. But it's called something else: a joint address to Congress. And it has its origins in the first term of President Ronald Reagan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump threatens increased tariffs if Canada retaliates
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday threatened to add additional taxes on Canada if the U.S.'s northern neighbor Canada retaliates with its own after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau slammed Trump’s newly implemented tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump wants to use the 'God Squad' to increase logging, but it must follow strict rules
item.description: President Donald Trump wants to increase logging in national forests and on public lands, including by bypassing endangered species protections.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wisconsin audit of Trump win finds not a single voting machine error
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — An audit of the November election won by President Donald Trump in swing-state Wisconsin found that not a single vote was counted incorrectly, altered or missed by tabulating machines.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, Fox 5
